lines: Norway are absolutely carrying half the sweepstake on their backs — Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all cashing in as they knocked out Côte d'Ivoire 1–0... sorry, 1–1, but the only numbers that matter ended 3–2 in Norway's favour. Five of you lot riding the same horse. Embarrassing lack of originality, frankly.

Meanwhile Al watched Netherlands bottle it against Morocco in penalties — 2–3 on the spot — so that's Al's best team gone, leaving him clinging to Croatia like a man on a liferaft made of wet cardboard. Morocco, by the way, is owned by Butters, Callum, Jim AND Wilmot. Al just watched them knock out his own team. Incredible scenes.

Ellis — currently 8th — wants you to know that Paraguay beat Germany on penalties yesterday, which is objectively mental. Germany. Out. To Paraguay. In a shootout. Fair play to the fuck, she's somehow still alive in this thing despite South Korea and New Zealand having fucked off home already.

Today's big one: France vs Sweden, and Callum, Croft, Dene, and The Foreman are all praying Les Bleus do the business against Jim's Sweden. The Foreman is propping up the table on 26pts and genuinely needs France to win every game between now and the final just to feel relevant — it's not looking great, mate.

Watch France vs Sweden closely — Jim (6th, 38pts) would absolutely love a Sweden upset to stick it to four rivals at once, and at this stage of the tournament that kind of chaos is very much on the table. Could be the most consequential match of the round for the sweepstake.
